column8: The Imperial Porcelain Factory has been making fine porcelain in Russia for centuries. The factory, founded in 1744, was Empress Elizabeth's idea with the aim of______________a centre of excellence in Russian ceramic Art. (CREATE)
column9: After Empress Elizabeth, consecutive monarchs kept the factory busy with orders and allowed it to produce the______________quality porcelain and ceramic plates, vases, tea sets and the like. (FINE)
column10: In the 1940s, the factory began to produce its famous cobalt net pattern, which consisted of intersecting lines of deep blue forming a geometric pattern on a white background. The edges of the dishes_____________in exquisite 22 karat gold. (HIGHLIGHT)
column11: Peter called inti his favourite burger shop on his way home from college one day. He didn't realise ha was out of cash until after he___________________his meal. (ORDER)
column12: The bill came to 4,99, and luckily, the burger shop accepted bank cards, so he paid with his card and went home. He____________his burger while he was watching TV, and then he went about the rest of his day. (EAT)
column13: A couple pf weeks later, Peter noticed a 499 charge on his bamk statement. "This charge can't be_____________. I haven't used my card for anything that expensive", Peter said aloud. (I)
column14: He saw the words "BURGER HUT" alongside the amount, so he called his bank and explained the situation. "It does seem strange that someone _________________that much money in a burger shop," the bank employee said, and he agreed to correct the charge. (SPEND)
